title: TSM Scene (v0.4)
description:
Renderer input. Authored figures (Path A) or algorithm-derived TSMs (Path B output). See ARCHITECTURE.md and the current SPEC-LENSES.md for spec.

v0.4 adds the emphasis-driven rendering layer (current SPEC-LENSES §3): `rendering.emphasis` is the visibility mechanism (primary | secondary; default render shows only primary, show-all reveals all); `rendering.lens` is the pedagogical naming tag from the canonical lens vocabulary (pure label, does not drive rendering). Scene-level `lenses` declares which lens entries this scene references.

Canonical lens vocabulary (6 entries, current SPEC-LENSES §2):
 - cyclic-flow: Backward transfers route work back to earlier stages by design.
 - coordinator-dispatch: A coordinator pushes work outward to many stations in parallel.
 - forward-handoff: Linear hand-off down the production line.
 - modularity-boundary: Module borders divide the diagonal into information-hiding zones.
 - cross-region-edge: A transfer crosses a modularity boundary.
 - core-periphery-boundary: A Core (largest cyclic group) sits with Control/Shared/Peripheral around it.

v1.6.4 D5.3 dropped the prior `label-only` lens. Items whose label IS the load-bearing claim (knife/money transfers, contract arrows, delivery/order transactions) now carry `rendering.labelStyle: "load-bearing"` — a display directive orthogonal to the lens vocabulary that never surfaces as an Explore chip (current SPEC-LENSES §3.5).

v0.3 scenes still validate (specVersion enum accepts both); the back-compat adapter at core/scene-adapter.js injects emphasis:primary on v0.3 transfers with rendering.arrow:true so existing scenes keep rendering identically.

specVersion.description: Schema version this scene targets. 0.3 = pre-lenses; 0.4.0 = current SPEC-LENSES (emphasis + lens vocabulary). The adapter back-fills emphasis:primary on arrowed transfers for 0.3 so older scenes render unchanged.

arrows.description: Scene-level cross-matrix arrows. Used by multi-matrix scenes (e.g. Fig 18.1 upstream-downstream) to encode arrows that route between two matrices — typically a contract or transaction crossing a firm boundary. Per-matrix intra-cell arrows still live on transfer.rendering.arrow (matrix.transfers[].rendering.arrow); this array is exclusively for inter-matrix endpoints.

lenses.description: Lenses referenced by items in this scene; derived from rendering.lens references if omitted. Order in the array determines Explore-mode chip display order. Each entry must be a canonical vocabulary id (see top-level description for the 6).

renderingEmphasis.description: Visibility tier. Default render shows only primary items; show-all reveals all. Current SPEC-LENSES §3.1. v0.3 scenes pass through the back-compat adapter which injects emphasis:primary on transfers with rendering.arrow:true so old scenes keep rendering identically.

emphasisCriteria.description: Selector criteria for walkthrough setEmphasis. See the current SPEC-LENSES §4. At least one of byLens/byId/byMark must be provided and at least one selector array must be non-empty — empty objects and empty selector arrays are silent no-ops at runtime, so the schema rejects them.

byLens.description: Canonical lens ids (any of the 6). Items whose rendering.lens contains any listed id match. V-LENS-5 validates vocabulary because JSON Schema leaves this string[] open for step-local diagnostics.

byId.description: Item identifiers. For transfers: 'from→to' (only matches when the transfer's rendering.arrow === true). For overlays: regionId for module-border overlays or 'firm-boundary:${overlayIndex}' for firm-boundary overlays. For annotations: annotation index (the annotation object has no id field in the current schema). For cross-matrix arrows: arrow.id or composite '${fromMatrix}:${fromTaskId}→${toMatrix}:${toTaskId}' fallback. Validator intentionally accepts any string here; existence depends on renderer-local keys and is not checked by V-LENS-6. See SPEC-LENSES §4.

byMark.description: Mark values (e.g. 'x', 's'). Targets transfers/marks by their mark field.

renderingLens.description: Optional pedagogical naming tag from the canonical vocabulary (one of 6; see schema top-level description). Pure label — does not drive rendering. Accepts a single string or an array of strings (e.g. a transfer carrying both coordinator-dispatch and cross-region-edge tags). Current SPEC-LENSES §3.2.

renderingLabelStyle.description: Display directive for items whose label IS the load-bearing claim (knife/money transfers in Fig 4, delivery/order transactions in Fig 6, contract arrows in Fig 18.1) rather than encoding a recurring concept that warrants a lens. Orthogonal to rendering.lens — labelStyle never participates in the lens vocabulary check, never surfaces as an Explore chip, and never drives emphasis or visibility. Renderers stamp the value to the DOM as `data-label-style` for CSS hooks and debugging only. Closed enum (currently "load-bearing" only); new values require a SPEC bump. Current SPEC-LENSES §3.5 (v1.6.4 D5.3).

setEmphasis.description: Current SPEC-LENSES §4: per-step emphasis transitions. Items matching `primary.byLens / byId / byMark` become rendering.emphasis:primary for this step; items matching `secondary.*` become secondary. Items not mentioned retain their JSON-declared emphasis. Reset between steps. At least one of primary/secondary must be provided — empty setEmphasis objects are silent no-ops at runtime, so the schema rejects them.

narrative.description: Optional per-matrix narrative. Resolution depends on mount mode. In single-matrix mounts (mountTSM), this takes precedence over scene.narrative for the mounted matrix — used by multi-matrix scenes (e.g. Fig 4 store-household, Fig 18.1 upstream-downstream) so a single-matrix view of the chosen matrix carries the matrix-specific walkthrough. In multi-matrix mounts (mountAllMatrices, side-by-side), matrix.narrative is DORMANT: the walkthrough fans scene.narrative out to all matrices as a unit and per-matrix narratives are not surfaced. mountAllMatrices logs a console.warn at mount when matrix.narrative is present so the dormancy is loud. Resolution chain: mountTSM = matrix.narrative ?? scene.narrative ?? null; mountAllMatrices = scene.narrative ?? matrix-0.narrative as a last-resort fallback.

shortLabel.description: Optional terse display label for the diagonal cell. The renderer shows this when present and falls back to label otherwise; the cell's title and aria-label attributes always carry the full label. Useful for dense matrices where the specific name overwhelms the partition shape. Empty strings are rejected because they would render a blank cell.

category.description: Optional free-form category label used to group tasks in a category-grouped legend or palette. Authors pick a vocabulary that fits the domain (e.g. cli/runtime/sdk/tools/mcp/coder for an agent system). The renderer does not interpret this field; it is consumed by legend/palette tracks. Mirrors dependency-observation node.category so derive() can round-trip the value onto the synthesized task.

kind.description: Visual styling hint. `transaction` is the contract/ecosystem arrow Fig 18.1 needs; `forward`/`backward` reuse the intra-matrix arrow palette.

matrix.description: Index into scene.matrices identifying which matrix the endpoint lives in.

taskId.description: Task id within the referenced matrix. The renderer anchors the arrow to that task's diagonal cell.
